subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall structure with recognisable sections (introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion)
Effective use of a table of contents and headings to guide the reader
Succinct title that captures the focus of the exploration
weaknesses: Some abrupt transitions between steps (e.g., deleting restaurants after three centroids) interrupt flow
Repetitive background material on veganism that does not advance the mathematics
Inconsistent formatting of figures, numbering, and decimal separators
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the report is logically developed and well organized in parts (band 3), redundant explanations and formatting inconsistencies limit conciseness (band 2). Overall the presentation meets basic organizational standards but falls short of a seamless, concise narrative, justifying a score of 2.

strengths: Use of maps, tables and successive Voronoi diagrams provides multiple representations
Standard notation for coordinates, gradients and equations is employed in key sections
Generally clear explanations of steps such as centroid and bisector construction
weaknesses: Inconsistent decimal notation and dropped vector arrows decrease clarity
Scales and legends are missing in figures, reducing interpretability
Some terms (e.g., âVoronoi vortexâ) and label conventions are ambiguous
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student employs some appropriate mathematical language and multiple representations (band 2), but inconsistencies in notation, unclear labels, and missing scale information prevent higher clarity and integration (band 3). A score of 2 reflects this middling performance.

strengths: Personal motivation (veganism) clearly drives the choice of topic
Use of geo-mapping tools (GeoGebra, Google Maps) shows personal initiative in data collection
weaknesses: Independent thinking is limited; mathematical steps follow standard procedures
Creativity beyond standard coursework (e.g., novel problem formulation) is minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is motivated by the studentâs own interests, but evidence of independent mathematical thinking and creative initiative remains limited (overall band 1), warranting a score of 1.

strengths: The conclusion paragraph briefly acknowledges limitations such as transport links
weaknesses: Reflection is superficial and descriptive, lacking deep critique of methods
No clear articulation of how the mathematics enhanced personal understanding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but remains very limited and descriptive, with only a basic acknowledgement of limitations (band 1). The absence of substantial critical analysis or connection to learning supports a score of 1.

strengths: Correct step-by-step application of the centroid formula
Accurate computation of midpoints serving as a sound basis for bisectors
weaknesses: Minor arithmetic and rounding errors (e.g., mis-sum of coordinates, inconsistent decimal usage)
Lacks deeper theoretical insight or sophisticated justification for choices such as number of sites
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevant SL-level mathematics is applied correctly in parts, but calculation inconsistencies and lack of theoretical depth limit the work to âsome appropriateâ rather than âeffectiveâ or âsophisticatedâ use (band 2). A score of 2 reflects this moderate level of mathematical use.

strandSelections.reasoning: The choice to study vegan restaurants and the selection of Voronoi diagrams show a personal interest, but mathematical decisions follow standard textbook procedures with little novel insight.  Evidence of independent thinking is therefore limited, matching band 1.

The student shows a sound grasp of how centroids and Voronoi cells are constructed, and explains each step, but deeper theoretical insight (e.g. why three sites suffice) is missing.  This reflects âgood understanding in partsâ, so band 3.

Calculations of midpoints, gradients and equations are mostly correct, but the slope between L and Q is rounded inaccurately and decimal notation is inconsistent.  These minor errors place the work in âmostly correctâ, i.e. band 3.

Most material is relevant to the research question, but the rationale contains lengthy background on veganism that does not advance the mathematics and some explanations are repeated (centroid definition, construction steps).  This fits âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ, i.e. band 2.

The student uses standard notation for coordinates, gradients and equations, but mixes commas and points for decimals, occasionally drops vector arrows and uses non-standard labels ("m of (LQ)").  This is âsome appropriateâ rather than âmostly appropriateâ use, so band 2.

Explanations generally make sense, but occasional ambiguities (e.g. âVoronoi vortexâ) and typographical errors disturb consistency.  Hence communication is âmostly clear with some consistencyâ, band 2.

Voronoi diagrams, centroids and perpendicular bisectors are all within the SL course and are applied correctly to a real data set, so mathematics is ârelevant at course levelâ.  The treatment is not extensive enough for âeffectiveâ or âsophisticatedâ, thus band 3.

Maps, tables and three successive Voronoi diagrams give visual support, yet scales are not always explained and the spreadsheet is pasted as plain numbers.  There are enough representations for âsome appropriate useâ but not the âeffectiveâ integration expected for band 3.
Reflection is confined to a short conclusion acknowledging limitations such as transport links; it remains superficial and descriptive.  This corresponds to âlimited reflectionâ, band 1.

Apart from overlaying Google Maps in GeoGebra and using a spreadsheet, there is little creativity or initiative beyond standard instructions.  Thus only âsomeâ creativity is evident, giving band 1.

The write-up follows a recognisable narrative (introduction, aim, methodology, construction, analysis, conclusion) and each part generally leads to the next, so the reader can follow the argument without undue effort.  Nevertheless several steps are abrupt â e.g. the jump from three centroids to deleting all other restaurants â and some passages are partially repetitive.  Overall logical development is clear but not exceptional, matching the descriptor for band 3.
There is a brief mention that other variables should be considered, but no probing critique of the mathematical method or results.  Hence only âbasic analysis with limited critiqueâ: band 1.
The student states that the mathematics helped to determine a location, but does not clearly articulate how this enhanced her mathematical understanding.  The link remains weak, fitting band 1.

A table of contents, headings and sub-headings are present, so the structure is easy to perceive.  Figures and tables are inserted where referenced, giving the report a sensible layout.  However inconsistencies such as mis-numbered figures, mixed decimal separators and reference formatting detract from a seamless flow, so the organisation is âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionallyâ so.

The student frames the exploration around her own dietary values and tailors data collection to MedellÃ­n, demonstrating a âclear personal approach that drives the explorationâ.  That aligns with band 2.

annotations.comment: Including both a map and a coordinate table provides useful multiple representations of data.
The centroid formula is applied step by step with correct substitution, demonstrating solid procedural understanding.
The selection of the closest site would be clearer if the student calculated distances using $$d=\sqrt{(x-x_c)^2+(y-y_c)^2}$$ for each candidate.
In the centroid of C calculation the sum of x-coordinates appears incorrect (used 8.84 instead of 8.87). Verify all arithmetic steps.
The midpoint of (LQ) is computed correctly, serving as a clear basis for the perpendicular bisector.
The gradient calculation m of (LQ)=6.7 omits the explicit division step. Show $$m=\frac{4.06-12.8}{7.58-8.87}$$ to enhance transparency.
The title succinctly captures the investigationâs focus and scope, providing a clear narrative entry point.
The course and assessment label uses mixed font styles and spacing. Unify formatting (e.g., âIA Mathematics Applications and Interpretations SLâ) for a cleaner presentation.
The table of contents heading is prominently placed and clearly signals structure, aiding reader navigation.
Several ToC entries split across lines (e.g., âAnalysis of the Voronoi diagramâ¦â). Ensure headings fit on one line or adjust column width to maintain coherence.
Page numbers are missing from the ToC. Adding them will improve usability and quick reference.
The rationale includes extensive background on veganism that does not directly advance the mathematical exploration. Consider condensing to focus on relevant context.
The studentâs personal journey into veganism as motivation demonstrates authentic engagement and sets a strong personal context.
The aim is clearly stated but could be more concise by removing quoted definitions and integrating them in the methodology.
The methodology section outlines steps but remains descriptive; it should specify the mathematical tasks, tools and criteria used in each phase.
The hypothesis is qualitative and lacks a predicted measure of coverage. Include a quantitative expectation (e.g., percentage area covered).
Outlining the use of GeoGebra and Google Maps in the exploration section demonstrates effective methodological planning.
Figure 1 lacks a scale bar and legend explanation. Add these details to improve interpretability of spatial data.
A coordinate entry â(8.87, 12,8)â uses inconsistent decimal separators. Standardize to â(8.87, 12.8)â throughout.
When presenting the perpendicular bisectorâs gradient (-0.149), include the rounding procedure (e.g., to three significant figures) explicitly.
Spreadsheet results use varying decimal separators and list a c-value of 61.5 for LE, which seems inconsistent. Check formatting and calculation.
Automating bisector equation generation in a spreadsheet shows creative initiative in data processing.
The description of creating Figure 2 would benefit from annotated labels on the exported image to clarify each edge.
Arithmetic steps to locate the new siteâs y-coordinate are clearly shown, reinforcing computational transparency.
The selection of the new siteâs x-coordinate is described verbally but lacks formal computation. Include the algebraic reasoning for clarity.
The figure caption is labeled âFigure 2â but follows a previous Figure 2. Ensure numbering consistency (should be Figure 3 here).
The conclusion section is clearly headed and frames the end of the report effectively.
The depth of reflection remains superficial, with limited critique of mathematical limitations. Develop more substantial critical analysis.
The first conclusion paragraph effectively summarizes the mathematical outcome and societal impact.
Reference entries duplicate URLs and lack consistent formatting. Adopt a single citation style and remove redundancies.
Google Maps entry would benefit from an access date and concise formatting to align with academic standards.
The reference list draws on reputable sources across dictionaries, databases and news outlets, demonstrating breadth of research.
